Requirements on Persistency
AUTOSAR AP Release 18-10
Table of Contents
1 Scope of this document 4
2 Requirements Specification 4
2.1 Functional overview : 4
2.2 Configuration of Persistent Data : 4
2.3 Storage of Persistent Data : 5
2.4 Secure Storage of Persistent Data : 6
2.5 Safe Storage of Persistent Data : 6
2.6 Installation, Update, Roll-back, and Removal of Persistent Data . . . . 7
2.7 Resource handling for Persistent Data : 9
3 Requirements Tracing 9
4 References 10
3 of 10
— AUTOSAR CONFIDENTIAL —Document ID 857: AUTOSAR_RS_PersistencyRequirements on Persistency
AUTOSAR AP Release 18-10
1 Scope of this document
This document specifies the requirements of Adaptive Applications to the functional
cluster Persistency of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. The motivation is to provide a
standardized and portable way to store and write data persistently.
2 Requirements Specification
2.1 Functional overview
The AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform Persistency provides services for Adaptive Applica-
tions and other clusters of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. The AUTOSAR Adaptive
Platform Persistency is responsible for all aspects which regard the storage/retrieve of
data and therefore it has to deal with:
Persistently store data over boot and ignition cycles
Load data which is persistently stored
Access data using a unique identifier
Read and write data from file-like structures
Encryption of persistent data
Error detection and correction of stored data
2.2 Configuration of Persistent Data
[RS_PER_00010] The layout of persistent data shall be configurable d
Type: draft
Description:Persistency shall support configuration of provided key-value data bases and
file arrays.
Rationale: Generation of interfaces
Dependencies:–
Use Case:An Adaptive Application or a Functional Cluster needs access to persistent
data and expects a dedicated interface for each set of data.
Supporting
Material:–
c(RS_Main_00440)
4 of 10
— AUTOSAR CONFIDENTIAL —Document ID 857: AUTOSAR_RS_Persistency
